channel.title: IBD LitMonitor - Quality of Life & PROs
channel.description: IBD literature monitoring: Quality of Life & PROs

item.title: Exposure to per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS) and development of autoimmunity in humans and animals: a scoping review.
item.description: Archives of toxicology - Review - PFAS are a group of persistent organic pollutants, that bioaccumulate and are associated with negative health effects. Reviews have suggested that the most critical effects of PFAS are on the immun...

item.title: The influence of C-reactive protein on depression in inflammatory bowel disease: A systematic review and meta-analysis.
item.description: Journal of affective disorders - The comorbidity between inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) and depression has been widely recognized, but the role of C-reactive protein (CRP) as a key inflammatory marker in this context is controve...

item.title: A Care Pathway for the Treatment of IBD Reduces Healthcare Costs and Is Cost-Effective: Results of the Multicentre IBD Value Study.
item.description: Alimentary pharmacology & therapeutics - An advanced therapy care pathway (ACP) for the treatment of patients with IBD can reduce practice variation and improve outcomes. This study aimed to estimate the effect of the ACP on costs and qua...

item.title: Effects of B vitamins and magnesium on fatigue, disease activity and quality of life in inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: Scientific reports - This study investigated the efficacy of supplementation with B vitamins and magnesium in alleviating fatigue, improving disease activity, and health-related quality of life (HRQoL) in patients with...

item.title: Types of physical activity and applied outcome measures in physical activity interventions among patients with inflammatory bowel disease: a scoping review.
Inulin-armored hesperetin‑zinc nanodrugs for synergistic redox-immune modulation and barrier repair in ulcerative colitis.
item.description: International journal of biological macromolecules - Ulcerative colitis (UC) is a chronic relapsing inflammatory bowel disease with a rising global incidence rate, which poses a significant challenge to clinical management due to the limited efficacy...
